suggestedNegativeKeywords.reasoning: Free-seeking shoppers rarely convert on a $29.99 premium product
Price-sensitive shoppers looking for budget options, not premium
Shoppers looking for replacement parts, not a new garlic press
Informational search intent, not purchase intent
Shoppers looking for electric models, not manual

referenceCampaigns.name: SP - Garlic Press - Exact Match
referenceCampaigns.reasoning: High-intent exact match keywords capture shoppers who know what they want. Dynamic up/down bidding optimizes for conversions. Negative keywords filter out low-value traffic.

referenceCampaigns.name: SP - Garlic Press - Broad/Phrase Discovery
referenceCampaigns.reasoning: Broad/phrase match keywords discover new search terms. Dynamic-up-only protects profitability by never raising bids above the set amount. Stronger negative keyword list to filter discovery traffic.

evaluationCriteria.name: Campaign Structure
evaluationCriteria.description: Campaign uses an appropriate type, targeting mode, and bid strategy for the product and goals.
checks: Campaign type is 'sponsored-products' (correct for a single ASIN product)
Bid strategy is dynamic (up-only or up-down) rather than legacy

evaluationCriteria.name: Keyword Selection
evaluationCriteria.description: Keywords are relevant, have appropriate match types, and cover both high-intent and discovery terms.
checks: At least 3 exact match keywords for high-intent targeting
At least 2 broad/phrase keywords for discovery
Keywords span different search intents (product-specific, use-case, quality)

evaluationCriteria.name: Negative Keywords
evaluationCriteria.description: Negative keywords effectively filter out irrelevant and low-converting search traffic.
checks: Negative keywords include irrelevant product types (e.g., 'electric')
Informational searches are negated (e.g., 'how to use')

evaluationCriteria.name: Bid Configuration
evaluationCriteria.description: Bids are set at appropriate levels relative to product margin, competition, and keyword intent.
checks: Default bid is within reasonable range ($0.50 - $2.00)
Exact match bids are higher than broad match bids
No bids exceed max profitable CPC (AOV x CVR x Target ACoS = $0.60)
Total daily spend fits within budget allocation

evaluationCriteria.name: Budget Allocation
evaluationCriteria.description: Daily budget is set appropriately for the product's sales velocity and goals.
checks: Daily budget is at least $10 (minimum for meaningful data collection)
Daily budget is at most $100 (appropriate for a $29.99 product)
Budget accommodates at least 10 clicks per day at the default bid

missionBrief.title: Campaign Architect Mission
missionBrief.scenario: You are launching a new Amazon PPC campaign for the Premium Garlic Press. This is a $29.99 product with a 35% profit margin. Your target ACoS is 25%, which means your break-even CPC is approximately $0.60 (AOV x CVR x Target ACoS). Your goal is to build a campaign structure that maximizes relevant traffic while staying profitable.
objectives: Choose the right campaign type and targeting mode for a single-ASIN product
Select high-relevance keywords with appropriate match types and bids
Add negative keywords to filter out irrelevant traffic
Set a daily budget that allows for meaningful data collection
Choose a bid strategy that balances visibility with profitability
tips: Sponsored Products is the right campaign type for individual ASINs — Sponsored Brands and Display are for different use cases
Manual targeting gives you keyword-level control; auto targeting lets Amazon decide
Exact match keywords have higher conversion rates but lower reach; broad match is the opposite
Dynamic bid strategies (up-only or up-down) generally outperform legacy fixed bids
Negative keywords are just as important as positive keywords — they prevent wasted spend
